theater_description: Single-city direct-impact episode centered on Hiroshima's urban core and nearby military installations struck at 08:15 on 1945-08-06. Civilian exposure is the resident civilian population physically present in the city; military exposure is the Japanese military presence physically in the city and directly affected installations.
geometry_method_note: No formal polygon used. Theater approximated as Hiroshima city proper / built-up urban area exposed to direct blast, heat, and ensuing fires on 1945-08-06.
military_area_note: Japanese Army presence in Hiroshima, especially Second Army headquarters and other military facilities within the city.
civilian_area_note: Resident civilians in Hiroshima on the bombing day; a wider civilian sensitivity bound uses a roughly 300,000 civilian-present estimate from DOE/Nuclear Museum style summaries.
split_reason: Different city, date, terrain, military presence, and source structure from Nagasaki. A paired whole-war denominator would be misleading.
quality_note: Good but imperfect triangulation: strong resident-civilian first-day series and explicit Hiroshima military deaths/presence exist, but military and civilian series come from different reconstruction traditions and foreign-worker/commuter counts remain unresolved.

theater_description: Single-city direct-impact episode centered on Nagasaki's Urakami Valley and adjoining urban-industrial districts struck at about 11:01-11:02 on 1945-08-09. Civilian exposure is the resident civilian population physically present in the city; military exposure is the Japanese military presence physically in Nagasaki on the attack day.
geometry_method_note: No formal polygon used. Theater approximated as Nagasaki city proper / the urban-industrial districts directly exposed to blast, heat, and ensuing fires on 1945-08-09.
military_area_note: Japanese military personnel physically present in Nagasaki on the bombing day, especially anti-aircraft and other local garrison elements.
civilian_area_note: Resident civilians in Nagasaki on the bombing day; a wider sensitivity bound uses the official city population figure of about 240,000.
split_reason: Different city, terrain, destruction pattern, and status mix from Hiroshima. Sources also report Nagasaki in a different structure than Hiroshima.
quality_note: Sparse/conflicting for the military split: civilian first-day estimates are available, but accessible sourcing for same-day Japanese military deaths in Nagasaki is weaker and relies on tertiary compilation.
